title: salix tyrrellii
intro: Salix tyrrellii, also known as Tyrrell's willow, is a species of willow tree that is native to the UK. The bark of this plant contains salicin, a compound that is similar to aspirin and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Salix tyrrellii include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as relieving symptoms of fever and colds. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including arthritis, headaches, and skin irritations.
